// SLIDE 01 — HOOK

CHAPTER 10 — WHAT NOT TO DELEGATE

Some tasks must not be delegated to Cowork regardless of how the brief is written or how capable the tool appears. The c

NARRATION

Some tasks must not be delegated to Cowork regardless of how the brief is written or how capable the tool appears. The categories: regulated data, sensitive personal records, personnel and legal judgment, irreversible actions, external commitments, and ambiguous or untrusted source material. The default posture when uncertain: refuse or redesign before starting, not after the output arrives. The decision rule: if the task involves sensitive data, regulated information, someone else's personal records, an irreversible action, or a judgment about a person , stop and redesign.

// SLIDE 02 — OPENING SCENE: THE TEMPTING RE

OPENING SCENE: THE TEMPTING REQUEST

You are a university staff member. A colleague sends you a folder of student accommodation files — disability documentat

Opening Scene: The Tempting Request — The question sounds reasonable. The folder is sitting right there. Cowork can read PDFs. You know roughly what categorie
NARRATION

You are a university staff member. A colleague sends you a folder of student accommodation files , disability documentation, medical letters, academic adjustment notes for thirty students. She says: "I need to categorize these by risk level so the advisor team knows who to prioritize. Can Cowork do this?" The question sounds reasonable. The folder is sitting right there. Cowork can read PDFs. You know roughly what categories mean. The task would take three hours manually.

// SLIDE 03 — WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO.

·Name the categories of tasks that must not be delegated to personal Cowork.
·Apply a concrete decision rule before starting a Cowork task.
·Distinguish "Cowork can do this" from "Cowork should do this."
Redesign a problematic task into a safer formor escalate it to the right channel.
·Recognize when "human review at the end" is not enough protection.
NARRATION

By the end of this chapter, you will be able to: Name the categories of tasks that must not be delegated to personal Cowork. Apply a concrete decision rule before starting a Cowork task. Distinguish "Cowork can do this" from "Cowork should do this." Redesign a problematic task into a safer form , or escalate it to the right channel. Recognize when "human review at the end" is not enough protection.

// SLIDE 04 — CORE CONCEPT: THE BOUNDARY OF

CORE CONCEPT: THE BOUNDARY OF SUPERVISED DELEGATION

Every previous chapter in this book has been about how to delegate well: how to prepare a workspace, how to write a brie

Core Concept: The Boundary of Supervised — Anthropic's guidance for Cowork is direct: sensitive data, sensitive apps, irreversible actions, and consequential judgm
NARRATION

Every previous chapter in this book has been about how to delegate well: how to prepare a workspace, how to write a brief, how to review a plan, how to check the output. This chapter argues that the discipline is incomplete without its complementary skill , knowing when not to delegate at all. Anthropic's guidance for Cowork is direct: sensitive data, sensitive apps, irreversible actions, and consequential judgment require extra care or a different approach entirely (Anthropic, "Use Claude Cowork safely") .

// SLIDE 05 — THE NONDELEGATION CATEGORIES

THE NONDELEGATION CATEGORIES.

Redesign if possible:→Redesign if possible:→Redesign if possible:→Redesign if possible:→Redesign for all irreversible actions:
NARRATION

Health records, medical documentation, treatment notes, insurance information, and anything that constitutes Protected Health Information (PHI) under U.S. law fall under HIPAA's Privacy Rule (HHS, "The HIPAA Privacy Rule"). Regulated clinical data belongs in approved clinical systems, not personal Cowork sessions. The rule is not "be careful with health data." The rule is: personal Cowork is not an approved system for PHI. If your organization has an approved enterprise AI workflow for health information, use that, with the governance it requires.

// SLIDE 06 — THE DECISION RULE

THE DECISION RULE.

·Identifiable health information about a person
·Student records or educational privacy-protected data
·Personnel evaluation, discipline, or promotion
·Legal advice, medical advice, or financial judgment
·Any action that cannot be undone without backup and human approval
NARRATION

Before starting any Cowork task, run it through this checklist: Stop and redesign if the task involves: Identifiable health information about a person Student records or educational privacy-protected data Personnel evaluation, discipline, or promotion Legal advice, medical advice, or financial judgment Any action that cannot be undone without backup and human approval An external commitment sent in your name or your organization's name Source material that is ambiguous, untrusted, or of unknown provenance Credentials, passwords, API keys, or authentication information

// SLIDE 07 — THREE REDESIGN MOVES

THREE REDESIGN MOVES.

Move 1: Reduce the data.→Move 2: Reduce the action.→Move 3: Increase governance.
NARRATION

When a task hits a stop condition, do not abandon the work , redesign it. Move 1: Reduce the data. Work with redacted, aggregated, anonymized, or synthetic versions of the source material. Instead of actual student records, use invented examples that have the same structure. Instead of real client contracts, use a generic template. Cowork can work effectively with stand-in data; it does not need the originals.

// SLIDE 08 — THE NONDELEGATION TABLE

THE NONDELEGATION TABLE.

Protected health data — Do not use personal Cowork
Student records — Do not use personal Cowork
Personnel decisions — Human-only judgment
Legal / financial / medical advice — Human professional
NARRATION

This section covers key concepts from the chapter.

// SLIDE 09 — WHY "HUMAN REVIEW AT THE END"

WHY "HUMAN REVIEW AT THE END" IS NOT ENOUGH.

The most common rationalization for proceeding with a problematic task is: "I will check the output before I do anything

Why "Human Review at the End" Is Not Eno — This reasoning fails for at least three reasons.
NARRATION

The most common rationalization for proceeding with a problematic task is: "I will check the output before I do anything with it." This reasoning fails for at least three reasons.

// SLIDE 10 — COMMON MISTAKES

COMMON MISTAKES

"If Cowork can do it, it is okay."→"The task is safe if the output stays on my computer."→"Redaction is always enough."→"Legal or medical disclaimers in the prompt make the output acceptable."→"Sensitive data only means passwords."
NARRATION

"If Cowork can do it, it is okay." Capability is not authorization. Cowork can read a student accommodation file. That does not mean it should. "The task is safe if the output stays on my computer." Cowork processes information to produce the output; the processing is the risk, not just the destination of the file.

// SLIDE 11 — BRIDGE TO CHAPTER 11

BRIDGE TO CHAPTER 11.

Chapters 9 and 10 together define the practical boundary of Cowork: what it can help assemble (knowledge artifacts, orga

Bridge to Chapter 11 — The discipline you have practiced across this book — defining the workspace, writing the brief, reviewing the plan, veri
NARRATION

Chapters 9 and 10 together define the practical boundary of Cowork: what it can help assemble (knowledge artifacts, organized notes, structured documents), and what must not be delegated to it. The next question is sustainability. Chapter 11 shows how to turn a successful, safe Cowork workflow into something repeatable , a standard operating procedure your team can use without starting from scratch each time.
